sector: Semiconductors — high-voltage power-conversion ICs (AC-DC, motor-drive, gate-driver, PowiGaN)
bull_thesis: POWI is a fortress-balance-sheet (~$250M cash & investments, ~zero debt), buyback-active (FY2025 $50M completed + new $50M authorized, ~1% dividend) IP franchise emerging from a deep channel-inventory correction with two distinct legs of growth. Leg 1 (here now): an industrial-led cyclical recovery — Industrial is 41% of Q1-26 revenue (up from 34% a year ago) and grew +23% YoY, with high-power (grid, rail, renewables, oil/gas) healthy and automotive 'doubling revenue this year'; channel weeks fell to 8.9 and days-on-hand to 292 (target <200), setting up operating leverage as volume normalizes. Q1-26 revenue $108.3M (+5% q/q, +3% YoY) landed in guide; Q2-26 guide $115-120M (+8.5% q/q) with non-GAAP GM stepping to 54-55% (+100bps). Leg 2 (the optionality): a genuine, differentiated GaN bet on the AI scale-up power transition. POWI's monolithic 1250V/1700V PowiGaN is technically ahead of stacked 650V GaN and competing 1200V SiC on density, it is collaborating with NVIDIA on 'a variety of sockets' in forthcoming 800VDC architectures, won 2 new aux-power design wins in Q1-26 at Taiwan customers serving US markets, and on May-31-2026 unveiled ultra-slim aux PSU reference designs purpose-built for NVIDIA's Kyber 800VDC liquid-cooled blade rack. Management quantifies a data-center SAM (rack + grid) '>$1B by 2030, bulk of it GaN'. If POWI converts even a modest share of 800VDC aux + main-power GaN sockets as megawatt racks proliferate, a ~$443M-revenue company adds a multi-hundred-million high-margin growth vector with structural AI-capex durability. Conservative, credible guider (lands inside its bands); clean accounting (the optical 290x GAAP P/E is a $6.2M-restructuring artifact, not run-rate). Conditions: (1) industrial recovery continues / channel finishes normalizing; (2) POWI wins production-grade (not just reference-design) 800VDC sockets in 2027-28; (3) GaN mix re-lifts gross margin back toward high-50s.
bear_thesis: At ~$87 near its 52-week high ($30.86-$89.00 range), POWI is priced on a 2027-2030 option that contributes ~zero revenue today, in a field that just got far more crowded. The numbers under the narrative are pedestrian: FY2025 revenue grew only +6% to $443.5M, Q1-26 was +3% YoY, GAAP EPS collapsed to $0.06 (from $0.15) on a $6.2M restructuring/workforce-reduction charge, and non-GAAP gross margin is drifting in the low-54s versus a historically high-50s franchise — the moat is not visibly widening. The entire AI-data-center thesis is forward optionality: main-power GaN is 'a couple years out' by management's own words; only small aux-PSU sockets are near-term. Worse, the 800VDC/Kyber arena is a multi-vendor brawl — TI announced a 'complete 800VDC power architecture with NVIDIA' (Mar-2026), and Navitas, onsemi, Infineon and STMicro are all chasing the same sockets — so POWI is one of several suppliers, not a sole-source, in its supposed crown-jewel market. The '>$1B SAM by 2030' is deliberately broadened to bundle grid/transmission gate-driver content into the AI headline, inflating the optics. Meanwhile the company is cutting heads ($6.2M restructuring) WHILE selling a secular-growth story — funding optionality with austerity. On any honest valuation the current base business (mid-single-digit grower, ~54% GM, ~$0.95-1.00 non-GAAP EPS run-rate, ~30x forward) is fully valued; the incremental ~$40-50/share of price is paying for a data-center win that is unbooked, deferred, and contested. Resolution driver: this name lives or dies on AI-capex durability — the SAME single macro factor as the entire scale-out basket — so it adds correlation, not diversification. What confirms the bear: a soft 2027 design-win cadence, GaN share ceded to TI/Navitas, or any AI-capex digestion scare de-rates the optionality fast.
sop_bull: Whole-company intrinsic (no reportable segment SoP; POWI guides one product line by end-market). Parts: (a) Core franchise (silicon AC-DC + industrial high-power + auto + legacy GaN): normalized non-GAAP EPS power ~$1.15-1.25 as channel clears and op-margin recovers to mid-teens-to-high-teens on $480-520M revenue; on a quality net-cash compounder multiple of ~32x = ~$37-40/share of operating value. (b) Net cash & investments ~$250M / ~55.7M shares = ~$4.50/share. (c) Data-center / 800VDC GaN optionality: credit a fuller capture of the >$1B-by-2030 SAM — assume POWI takes ~$250-300M of incremental high-margin (GaN-rich, ~60%+ GM) data-center revenue by 2030, NPV/risk-adjusted back to a present per-share contribution of ~$22-26. Sum: ~$37-40 + ~$4.50 + ~$22-26 = ~$64-70 base, but with full-conviction data-center execution and GM re-lift the bull tags ~$98-105/share.
sop_bear: Parts: (a) Core franchise valued as a mid-single-digit cyclical grower with a flat-to-eroding ~54% GM: ~$0.95-1.00 normalized non-GAAP EPS x a de-rated 26-28x (a quality semi but NOT a hyper-grower) = ~$26-28/share. (b) Net cash ~$4.50/share. (c) Data-center optionality heavily haircut for the crowded multi-vendor 800VDC field (TI/Navitas/onsemi/Infineon/STMicro) and the 'couple-years-out' deferral — credit only ~$8-12/share for aux-PSU wins + a small GaN share. Sum: ~$26-28 + ~$4.50 + ~$8-12 = ~$39-45/share. If AI-capex digests and the optionality de-rates entirely, downside toward the high-$30s/low-$40s — a ~50%+ drawdown from $87.
sop_breakdown: Core operating franchise (industrial-led recovery, ~54% GM, normalized ~$1.05-1.15 non-GAAP EPS x ~30x quality net-cash semi multiple) ~$33-36/share + net cash & investments ~$250M (~$4.50/share) + risk-adjusted 800VDC/GaN data-center optionality (real PowiGaN density edge + NVIDIA Kyber alignment + 2 aux wins, but multi-vendor field and 2027-30 deferral) ~$18-23/share = ~$56-64. Base ~$61. The current ~$87 price embeds ~$25+/share of data-center capture that is unbooked, deferred, and contested — i.e., a meaningful chunk of the AI-scale-out optionality is already in the price.
risk_reward: Unfavorable at $87. Upside-to-base-fair (~$61) is NEGATIVE ~-30%; even the bull SoP (~$98-105) is only ~+13-21% and requires full data-center execution. Downside-to-bear (~$39-45) is ~-48% to -55%. Risk/reward is skewed to the downside (~0.4:1 reward:risk to base) because the optionality is already substantially priced; entry asymmetry only turns favorable on a washout into the $50s.
catalyst_status: SOFT_EXTENDED. Load-bearing catalyst = conversion of NVIDIA 800VDC/Kyber design-ins into booked production revenue. Dated evidence: Oct-2025 OCP white paper (1250V/1700V PowiGaN for 800VDC); 2 aux-power design wins disclosed Q1-26 (call ~May-2026) at Taiwan customers; May-31-2026 ultra-slim aux PSU reference designs for NVIDIA Kyber. These are real and dated BUT (a) reference designs / aux sockets are small near-term, (b) management explicitly puts main-power GaN 'a couple years out' (2027-28), (c) the 800VDC field is multi-vendor (TI's full-architecture-with-NVIDIA announced Mar-2026; Navitas/onsemi/Infineon/STMicro competing). No hard production award, no quantified data-center revenue, no book-to-bill inflection. The catalyst is genuine but serially-extending and contested = SOFT_EXTENDED, not PENDING_HARD.
peer_comps_note: True peers = the AI/data-center power-semi cohort. Live multiples (verified ~2026-06-21): Monolithic Power (MPWR) — the gold-standard comp — ~$76.8B cap, ~$1,565 price, trailing P/E ~113x, forward P/E ~50-59x, NTM EV/EBITDA ~49x (vs peer median ~21x); record Q1-26 revenue $804M (+26% YoY) — i.e., MPWR is GROWING ~5x faster than POWI and trades richer, but its data-center revenue is already material. onsemi (ON) — forward P/E ~32x, AI-data-center revenue +30% q/q and >2x YoY (already booked), priced as a value-inflection. Navitas (NVTS) — pure-play GaN/SiC, no P/E (loss-making), forward EV/Sales ~10-24x; the high-beta optionality proxy. POWI (~$4.85B cap, ~$87) trades at ~30x forward / ~290x trailing GAAP (restructuring-distorted) on only ~3-6% growth and ~54% GM. RANKING within the cohort: VALUATION — POWI mid-pack on forward P/E (cheaper than MPWR, similar to ON, less speculative than NVTS) but expensive for its growth; GROWTH — LOWEST of the cohort (low-single-digit vs MPWR +26%, ON's booked DC ramp); MARGINS — high gross margin (~54%) but flat/eroding and below its own history, op-margin depressed (13.5-15.5%); RETURNS — currently depressed (restructuring), net-cash quality balance sheet is the standout; AI-CAPEX EXPOSURE — REAL but the LEAST-MONETIZED of the cohort (POWI's DC revenue ~zero today / 2027-30 ramp, vs MPWR & ON already shipping). Conclusion: POWI is the 'earliest-innings, lowest-current-growth, cleanest-balance-sheet, most-optionality-dependent' name in PWR-SEMI — it screens neither cheap-for-its-growth nor a proven DC winner yet.
role_in_scaleout: POWI's role is high-voltage power conversion at the rack edge of the 800VDC AI data-center transition — specifically auxiliary PSUs (1700V PowiGaN flyback for NVIDIA Kyber housekeeping power) today and, prospectively, main-power GaN switches for 800VDC megawatt racks plus grid/transmission gate-drivers; its monolithic 1250V/1700V GaN density edge is genuinely differentiated, but the demand is durable-but-deferred (2027-2030) and contested by TI/Navitas/onsemi/Infineon, so it is a credible participant — not a sole-source bottleneck — in the power layer.
consensus_delta: The street is NOT looking the wrong way in POWI's favor — if anything the optionality is over-discovered. The bull-side consensus assumption baked into the ~$87 price (near 52-week high, ~30x forward) is that POWI converts a meaningful slice of the '>$1B-by-2030 data-center SAM' into high-margin GaN revenue. The factual problems with that assumption: (1) data-center revenue is ~ZERO today — the entire premium is forward optionality; (2) management itself says main-power GaN is 'a couple years out' — the near-term is only small aux-PSU sockets; (3) the 800VDC field is multi-vendor (TI announced a full 800VDC architecture WITH NVIDIA in Mar-2026; Navitas/onsemi/Infineon/STMicro all competing) so the 'NVIDIA collaboration' is shared, not exclusive; (4) the '>$1B SAM' bundles grid/transmission content into the AI headline to inflate it. The genuine consensus delta that is UNDER-appreciated cuts the other way: the base business is only a +3-6% grower with a FLAT-to-eroding ~54% gross margin (below its own high-50s history) and a $6.2M restructuring/headcount cut — i.e., the 'AI power play' is funding optionality with austerity while the core compounds slowly. Expectations are saturated, not mispriced low.
valley_of_death: Near-term (3-9mo) risks that hit BEFORE the data-center thesis can pay: (1) AI-capex digestion scare — POWI's premium is pure AI-capex optionality, so any hyperscaler capex guide-down or monetization wobble (the regime doc flags AI-capex as ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth and the dominant shared factor) de-rates the optionality immediately and disproportionately; (2) consumer/appliance weakness persists — Consumer fell to 38% of mix and remains the drag; a softer appliance/AC season pressures the blended growth/GM; (3) gross-margin disappointment — if the guided +100bps GM step (to 54-55%) stalls, the 'GaN-lifts-margin' sub-thesis is questioned; (4) competitive design-loss headlines — a visible TI/Navitas Kyber socket win would puncture POWI's narrative; (5) GAAP optics — continued restructuring keeps reported EPS thin and the ~290x trailing P/E a recurring bear talking point. No debt-maturity or cash-burn risk (fortress balance sheet, FCF-positive: Q1-26 FCF $18M), so the valley is a MULTIPLE/sentiment valley, not a solvency one.
positioning_washout: No dividend-cut or forced-passive-liquidation dynamic — the ~1% dividend is well-covered and stable, and the shareholder base is growth/quality-semi holders, not yield investors. The washout risk is the opposite: POWI has run from ~$31 to ~$87 (near 52-week high) on the AI-power narrative, so it carries momentum/AI-thematic ownership that is mechanically VULNERABLE to a de-grossing event. If AI-capex sentiment cracks, thematic and quant-momentum holders sell first and fastest, and a low-single-digit-grower trading at ~30x forward has little fundamental floor until the high-$30s/low-$40s (bear SoP) — that is the capitulation level a patient buyer should wait for. There is no near-term mechanical forced-seller catalyst to exploit on the long side.
forcing_function: The re-rating triggers are (a) POWI's Q2-26 earnings (next print, ~early Aug-2026) — watch for any QUANTIFIED data-center revenue, a book-to-bill/aux-socket cadence, and whether GM steps to the guided 54-55%; (b) the 2027 800VDC production-award cycle — the first PRODUCTION (not reference-design) Kyber/main-power GaN socket win or loss, which is the true binary and is ~12-24 months out; (c) NVIDIA's own 800VDC/Kyber ramp timeline and any named power-IC supplier disclosures; (d) competitive milestones from TI/Navitas/onsemi that could reallocate the socket map. None of these is a hard-dated, binding, asymmetric near-term catalyst — they are a slow design-win drip — which is precisely why this is a WATCH, not an ENTRY.
moderator_conclusion: POWI is a high-quality, fortress-balance-sheet (~$250M net cash, buyback-active, FCF-positive) IP franchise with a GENUINE technical edge (monolithic 1250V/1700V PowiGaN) and a REAL seat at the 800VDC/NVIDIA-Kyber table — but at ~$87, near its 52-week high, the market has already capitalized a large slice of a 2027-2030 data-center option that contributes ~zero revenue today, while the base business grows only low-single-digit on a flat-to-eroding ~54% gross margin. Applying the regime (CATALYST_WATCH_REGIME §3/§4: complacent melt-up, AI-capex the load-bearing wall and dominant SHARED factor, book-independence enforced hard, hard-binary/run-up catalysts to be PENALIZED): POWI fails three of the regime's discipline tests. (1) Its load-bearing catalyst is SOFT_EXTENDED, not hard-dated — reference designs and small aux sockets, with main GaN 'a couple years out' in a multi-vendor field (TI's full 800VDC-with-NVIDIA architecture, Mar-2026, is the warning shot). (2) It has ALREADY RUN ~180% off the low — the optionality is over-discovered, not offside. (3) Its resolution driver is AI-capex durability — the EXACT shared macro factor as the rest of scale-out Basket D — so it adds correlation, not independence; the regime says penalize that. Reconciled base SoP ~$61 sits ~30% BELOW the price; risk/reward is ~0.4:1 (downside to bear ~$40 of -50%+ vs upside to base negative). value_conviction is also 2 (not a moat-cap issue — the moat is narrow but not eroding terminally, secular_threat none — purely a valuation issue: a slow-growing ~54%-GM franchise at ~30x forward + a contested unbooked option is fully-to-richly valued, not a value entry). VERDICT C / conviction 2: PASS at $87. This is a quality name to OWN on a washout — the WATCH-for-capitulation level is the high-$30s/low-$40s on an AI-capex de-gross — but not to chase here. Within PWR-SEMI, onsemi (booked DC ramp, ~32x) and MPWR (proven DC franchise) offer more monetized exposure; POWI is the earliest-innings, lowest-current-growth, most-optionality-dependent seat in the basket.
interrogator_dossier:
# Forensic Dossier — Power Integrations, Inc. (NASDAQ: POWI)
*Interrogator pass · Opus 4.8 · 2026-06-22 · scale-out Basket D (PWR-SEMI) · web-sourced (no bundled transcripts)*

**Snapshot:** Price ~$87.09 (2026-06-21) · Market cap ~$4.85B · FY2025 revenue $443.5M (+6% YoY) · FY2025 GAAP EPS $0.39 · Non-GAAP GM 54.5% · ~$250M cash & investments, ~zero debt · Q1-26 rev $108.3M (+3% YoY) · Q2-26 guide $115-120M.

Methodology note: no multi-quarter transcript bundle was provided; reconstructed from web-sourced quarterly results (Q3-25 -> Q1-26 / FY2025), the Q1-26 call, and POWI/NVIDIA press releases. Multi-quarter tone arc is flagged where it cannot be sourced verbatim and reasoned from the financial trajectory.

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC
Entered the window as a cyclical-bottom recovery story (high-voltage power-IC franchise digesting a deep channel-inventory correction; bull case = inventory normalization + industrial/auto content growth + a pending GaN ramp; consumer/appliance the drag). Across FY2025 -> Q1-26 the story EXPANDED and re-centered on the AI data center: Oct-2025 OCP Summit detailed 1250V/1700V PowiGaN for 800VDC; Q1-26 call quantified a 'data center SAM, including rack and grid, >$1B by 2030, bulk being GaN'; May-31-2026 unveiled ultra-slim aux PSU reference designs for NVIDIA Kyber. Meanwhile the base business quietly re-mixed to industrial (41% of Q1-26 from 34%, +23% YoY) while consumer fell to 38% (from 44%). A leadership handoff (founder Balakrishnan -> Lloyd CEO-voice / Erba CFO) lands as the thesis pivots. Net arc: from 'wait for the inventory cycle' to 'industrial-led recovery + credible GaN/AI-data-center optionality that is real but largely 2027-2030.'

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS
- Recovery/orders improving: SUPPORTED but modest (Q1-26 +5% q/q, +3% YoY; FY2025 only +6%). A recovery, not a boom.
- Industrial is the engine (+23% YoY, 'main driver'): SUPPORTED, corroborated by the 34%->41% mix shift; auto 'doubling.' This is the genuine current growth.
- Data-center SAM >$1B by 2030, bulk GaN: NARRATIVE RICHER THAN NUMBERS — zero quantified DC revenue today; only 2 aux design wins; main GaN 'a couple years out.' Optionality, not earnings power.
- Margin recovery: MIXED — non-GAAP GM low-54s (vs historical high-50s), drifting not expanding; GAAP Q1-26 net income $3.3M / EPS $0.06 (from $8.8M/$0.15) on a $6.2M restructuring charge; reported ~290x P/E is a GAAP artifact.
- Capital discipline: SUPPORTED — FY2025 $50M buyback completed + new $50M auth; ~$250M net cash, ~zero debt; ~1% dividend. Genuine net-cash return-of-capital profile.
- OpEx discipline: SUPPORTED but the workforce reduction is a defensive tell.
Story>numbers: the data-center/GaN TAM. Numbers>story: the industrial mix and the balance sheet.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY
Rising (cautiously) on data-center vision; Stable-to-defensive on the core P&L. Offensive language clusters on the new vector ('huge market,' '>$1B SAM,' 'variety of sockets' with NVIDIA, auto 'doubling'); defensive language persists on legacy ('macro uncertainty,' appliance weakness, tariff digestion) plus a restructuring/workforce cut in the same window — the late-cycle 'huge TAM + we cut costs' pairing. Guidance is precise on the quarter and deliberately vague on data-center timing ('a couple years') — the honest tell of where the money actually is.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY
Medium-High. Conservative narrow-range guider that lands inside its bands (Q4-25 $100-105M; Q1-26 $108.3M inside $104-109M; Q2-26 $115-120M credible). The credibility TENSION is the '>$1B by 2030 SAM' framing — discount as vision, not guidance. No sandbagging; honest that DC is back-half-of-decade; GAAP EPS volatility (restructuring) muddies the headline but is disclosed cleanly.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS
'When does data center show up in revenue?' (recurring non-answer -> 'a couple years'); gross-margin ceiling (no path back to high-50s articulated); channel inventory (days-on-hand <200 target, slow grind); competitive encroachment in 800VDC (TI/Navitas/onsemi/Infineon/STMicro on the same Kyber sockets) — least-quantified by management.

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS
RED: restructuring/workforce cut ($6.2M) while selling a growth story; thin/volatile GAAP earnings (EPS $0.06; ~290x P/E artifact); data-center revenue ~zero today ('couple years out'); crowded multi-vendor 800VDC field; stock already run ($31->$87, near high).
GREEN: fortress balance sheet (~$250M cash, ~zero debt, buyback, ~1% dividend); genuine industrial recovery + mix upgrade (41%, +23% YoY, auto doubling); differentiated PowiGaN (1250V/1700V monolithic ahead of stacked 650V GaN / 1200V SiC); real NVIDIA Kyber alignment; channel cleaning up.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS
Vocabulary migration: 'auxiliary power' (small, near-term, winnable) vs 'main power / high-voltage GaN' (large, 2027-2030) — big dollars deferred. 'SAM including rack AND grid' bundles grid/transmission gate-driver content into the AI headline to enlarge the number. Leadership handoff lands as the thesis pivots; new voices more DC-forward. Capital-allocation tone shifts subtly toward growth capex (5-6% of revenue, H2-weighted) vs pure return-of-capital.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT
Trajectory STABLE -> PIVOTING: core stable-to-recovering; equity thesis pivoting to a GaN/AI-data-center optionality that is genuine but back-half-of-decade. The director's key weigh: how much of a 2030 SAM is already capitalized at ~$87 (near 52-week high, ~30x fwd / ~290x trailing GAAP) when DC revenue is ~zero and the big GaN sockets are 'a couple years out' in a crowded field. Moat: intangible/IP + design-in switching-cost, NARROW — non-GAAP GM flat-to-eroding in the low-54s (vs high-50s history), returns currently depressed, premium rests on a forward option not expanding present economics; the new growth arena is MORE competitive than the legacy niche. Moat trend STABLE (lean eroding on margin). Secular threat NONE — POWI is on the RIGHT side of AI-capex/electrification/grid; terminal risk is competitive/cyclical and valuation (paying for a 2030 win today), not structural decline. Not cheap; a fairly-to-fully-priced optionality.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STABLE | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: none
skeptic_kill_fact: No kill_fact found. Every adversarial attack vector was tested and the bear case held against primary sources, so the debate's bearish PASS-at-$87 verdict (C, conviction 2) survives. (a) STALE-ANCHOR: the $58-64 SoP is built on CURRENT financials — FY2025 rev $443.5M/+6% and Q1-26 $108.3M are the latest reported, not pre-event. (b) NUMBER TRUTH: all load-bearing figures verify — FY2025 rev $443.5M (+6%), GAAP EPS $0.39, Q1-26 rev $108.3M (+3% YoY), Q2-26 guide ~$117.5M (+8.5%), new $50M buyback after prior $50M completed, fortress net-cash balance sheet. (c)/(d): the crown-jewel AI claim is the WEAKEST link and it confirms the bear — POWI is NOT sole-source (TI unveiled a COMPLETE 800VDC architecture WITH NVIDIA at GTC, 2026-03-16, per TI's own newsroom), near-term DC content is only 15W/35W aux 'control and housekeeping' PSUs (DER-1110/DER-1114, BusinessWire 2026-05-31), main-power GaN is 'a couple years out' per management, and DC revenue is ~zero today. The premium at the 52-week high pays for an unbooked, deferred, contested option — exactly the bear's claim.
skeptic_corrections: Q1-26 non-GAAP gross margin was 53.5% (per the Q1-26 call), NOT the 'low-54s/54.5%' stated in several places in the debate (sop, interrogator). This is a MINOR correction that strengthens the bear (margin is even softer vs the high-50s history) and does not change the verdict. Also: total restructuring charges cited on the call were ~$6.6M, not $6.2M (immaterial). FY2025 GAAP EPS is $0.39 (correctly noted in the dossier; one bull-thesis line implies a different prior-year comp but the $0.39 figure is right).
skeptic_evidence: TI.com newsroom, 2026-03-16: 'TI unveils complete 800 VDC power architecture for future generation AI data centers with NVIDIA' (GTC Mar 16-19, 2026) — confirms POWI is one of several NVIDIA 800VDC power suppliers, not sole-source.

BusinessWire, 2026-05-31: 'Power Integrations Unveils Space-Saving, Ultra-Slim Auxiliary PSU Reference Designs for NVIDIA Kyber 800 VDC' — DER-1110 (15W) / DER-1114 (35W) for 'control and housekeeping'; confirms near-term DC content is small aux sockets, not main power.

Power Integrations FY2025 results (Morningstar/BusinessWire & SEC 8-K, 2026-02-05): full-year revenue $443.5M (+6%), GAAP net income $22.1M / $0.39 diluted EPS, CFO $111.5M, new $50M buyback after completing prior $50M — verifies the fortress-balance-sheet and base-growth figures.

POWI Q1-26 earnings call (2026-05, via Investing.com transcript): mgmt — 'data center SAM, including rack and grid applications, will exceed $1 billion by 2030'; main-power GaN 'not next year... in a couple years'; non-GAAP gross margin 53.5%, Q2 guide 54-55%; 2 new aux design wins at Taiwan customers.

scale.role: GaN aux-power for NVDA Kyber 800VDC reference designs; design-ins not yet booked production.
scale.rationale: Real aux-socket design-ins (DER-1110/DER-1114 for Kyber) but they are reference designs / 15-35W aux 'housekeeping' sockets, not the main 800V power, with near-zero DC revenue today and main-power GaN 'a couple years out' — a speculative TACTICAL franchise. Skeptic verdict CONFIRMED_WITH_CORRECTIONS, corrections minor and bear-reinforcing (gross margin even softer; one of several NVIDIA suppliers, not sole-source), ~8pt haircut off a ~43 franchise. Base ~$61 is ~30% below spot; wait.
scale.demand_durability: Medium — aux-power design-ins are early and not the primary 800V socket; broad-consumer base.
